dashboard_meta.architecture: Layer 1 agents are sealed independent raw signal producers. No cross-agent communication.

agents.summary: USD Layer 1 raw call generated from sealed independent workflow.

24h.reason: USD shows a bullish lean with strong support from primary drivers including Fed bias, US 2Y yield rise, and positive US economic data. However, bearish signals from the US-DE 2Y spread narrowing and DXY 5-day delta weakness create some conflict. The low VIX and Dollar Smile regime input also moderate the bullish conviction, resulting in a lean rather than a full bullish call.

F5 DXY 5-Day Delta: BEARISH — DXY down -0.41% over 5d (DXY weakness confirms USD bearish momentum)
F6 Gold 5-Day Delta: NEUTRAL — Gold flat with negligible 5d change (Gold stable, no USD directional evidence from gold)
F2 US 2Y Yield Delta: BULLISH — US 2Y yield +6 bps over 5d (Significant US 2Y yield rise supports USD)
F9 Dollar Smile Regime: BEARISH — VIX low, risk-on regime, US growth not strongly outperforming (Dollar Smile regime suggests USD weakness in risk-on growth scenario)
F3 US/DE 2Y Spread Delta: BEARISH — US-DE 2Y spread narrowed by 5 bps over 5d (Narrowing spread weakens USD versus Euro)
F4 US 10Y Real Yield Delta: NEUTRAL — US 10Y real yield +5 bps over 5d (borderline) (Real yield rise just below bullish threshold, scored neutral)
F7 US Economic Surprise Direction: BULLISH — NFP data beat consensus (actual 172k vs forecast 85k) (Positive US economic surprise supports USD)
F10 Equity Direction vs USD Correlation Regime: BULLISH — Risk-on regime with equities falling (-4.8% NQ 5d) (Equities falling in risk-on regime supports USD)
